title: Pain functional impact review

examination.text: Supports pain severity, daily activity, sleep, participation, physical and psychological wellbeing, medicine response and adverse effects, coping, clinician-led treatment planning, early and regular review, and specialist referral without mapping medicine names or doses.
examination.rationale: Supports daily activity, sleep, participation, physical and psychological wellbeing and coping review for pain functional impact review; no decision is generated.
examination.evidence_extract: Supports pain severity, daily activity, sleep, participation, physical and psychological wellbeing, medicine response and adverse effects, coping, clinician-led treatment planning, early and regular review, and specialist referral without mapping medicine names or doses.

source.title: Neuropathic pain in adults: pharmacological management in non-specialist settings

source_limitations: All item-level mappings remain unsupported pending separate clinician review and signed approval.
Pain diagnosis and site, personal roles, mobility, self-care, work, sleep, goals, measures, supports, barriers and agreed plan are absent.
No diagnosis, interpretation, investigation, treatment, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, escalation or disposition is generated for pain functional impact review.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
